key_insight: Latent Drift forecasts slow disease progression by modeling subtle anatomical change in a compressed latent space instead of trying to regenerate full MRI volumes directly.
clinical_relevance: This could support earlier detection of neurodegenerative progression, more targeted intervention planning, and better design of longitudinal clinical trials.
method: Learn a latent representation of progression rather than predicting pixel-level anatomy.
Use Finite Scalar Quantization to reduce nuisance noise and preserve consistent drift.
Compare against diffusion and autoregressive transformer baselines for patient-specific forecasting.
evidence_signal: On longitudinal 3D brain MRI, the method improved generative fidelity and clinically relevant metrics over diffusion and autoregressive transformer baselines.
watch_item: Because it is trained on subtle longitudinal MRI changes, performance may depend on data quality, scan consistency, and careful validation before real-world deployment.
paper_title: Progression as Latent Drift: Generative Forecasting of Slow-Evolving Pathologies
